Eunseo Choi's Homepage
About CV Publications

CV

CV

Download a PDF version.

Education

  • Ph.D. in Geophysics, 2008, California Institute of Technology.
  • B.Sc. in Geology, 1999, Seoul National University.

Research Interest

  • Long-term lithospheric deformations in various tectonic environments including mid-ocean ridges and continental rifts.
  • Advanced numerical techniques for modeling very large and multiscale deformations in geodynamic problems

Past and Present Projects

  • Coupling a tectonic modeling code, SNAC, to a surface modeling code, CHILD (2011-).
  • Landslide modeling (2012-).
  • A novel modeling code development (DynEarthSol2D) (2011-2012).
  • Sequential rider block formation (2011-2012).
  • 3D Basin formation at bends of strike-slip faults (2010).
  • Constraints on shallow mantle viscosity from ridge axial morphology and deformation (2009-2010).
  • Geometry of the ridge-transform fault intersection (2005-2007).
  • Spacing of fracture zones in oceanic lithosphere (2006-2007).
  • SNAC development. Co-working with VPAC in Australia and Lavier at U. Texas (2003-2006).
  • Geoframework: Developing a framework to couple component codes and applying it to modeling crust-mantle interactions (2003-2007).
  • Detecting surface deformation in Iceland using the InSAR technique. Advisor: Prof. Mark Simons at California Institute of Technology.
  • Modeling the extension of the Basin and Range province.

Publications

Link to publication list

Grants and Computational Resources

  • 2014.09, Earth Cube Building Blocks: Earth System Bridge. (PI: Peckham et al.)
  • 2012.09, Landslide dynamics from seismic wave inversion, satellite remote sensing, and numerical modeling, EAR 12-27083.
  • 2010.06, TeraGrid Research Allocation (EAR100019): 1.8 MSU.
  • 2009.06, 3D Models of Faulting during Oblique Continental Extension, NSF EAR-0911565.

Honors and Scholarship

  • Conoco Fellowship at California Institute of Technology (2000-2001)
  • Graduated summa cum laude from Seoul National University (1999)
  • Scholarship for the distinguished students, Seoul National University (1996-1999)
